standard_metric.name: Total processed tokens
standard_metric.definition: Use total input plus output tokens as the primary inference-demand metric. When a provider discloses token throughput instead of totals, track tokens per minute separately. When only messages are disclosed, estimate token-equivalents with a clearly labeled range.
standard_metric.why_it_matters: Tokens processed is the cleanest bridge between user demand, model throughput, infrastructure load, and provider monetization.

metric_stack.name: Direct processed tokens
metric_stack.description: Closest to actual inference compute served. Prefer monthly or weekly totals from provider disclosures or marketplace rankings.

metric_stack.name: Token throughput
metric_stack.description: Useful when companies publish tokens per minute or similar throughput rates instead of period totals.

metric_stack.name: Messages or requests
metric_stack.description: A strong demand read-through when token totals are not available, but requires a token-per-message assumption.

metric_stack.name: Users, seats, and customers
metric_stack.description: Shows how broadly inference products are diffusing across consumer and enterprise surfaces.

metric_stack.name: Revenue, capex, and utilization
metric_stack.description: Confirms that token growth is translating into spending power and infrastructure demand.

kpis.label: OpenRouter monthly tokens
kpis.note: Current platform-wide monthly volume on OpenRouter's public rankings/state-of-AI pages.

kpis.label: Gemini monthly tokens
kpis.note: Google said Gemini was processing more than 980 trillion monthly tokens by July 2025.

kpis.label: OpenAI daily messages
kpis.note: OpenAI research reported 2.627 billion daily ChatGPT messages in June 2025.

kpis.label: OpenAI weekly users
kpis.note: OpenAI said ChatGPT had more than 800 million weekly users by November 2025.

comparison_rows.weekly_growth: Starts once snapshot archive is live
comparison_rows.monthly_growth: Starts once monthly archive is live
comparison_rows.note: Best transparent marketplace tape, but not the whole industry.
Cleanest big-lab token disclosure currently available.
Direct token disclosure is sparse, so scale is inferred from messages and users.
Likely much larger in reality, but first-party total token figures are not public.

tracking_plan.coverage: Top models, author share, top apps, and total marketplace token flow

tracking_plan.focus: Direct provider token and throughput disclosures
tracking_plan.coverage: Gemini monthly tokens, OpenRouter monthly totals, any new lab token disclosures

tracking_plan.focus: Users, seats, customers, capex, and revenue read-throughs
tracking_plan.coverage: OpenAI users and seats, hyperscaler capex, cloud AI monetization signals
OpenAI usage papers, Anthropic Economic Index, marketplace retrospectives

provider_snapshots.measurement: Direct tokens and model-level weekly usage
provider_snapshots.headline: Weekly token volumes by model and app are publicly visible.

current_metric.label: Public monthly volume
current_metric.growth: Need weekly snapshots to compute platform WoW and MoM cleanly.

bullets: Best publicly accessible weekly token leaderboard across frontier and open models.
Useful as a live inference-demand tape even though it represents marketplace traffic rather than the whole industry.
Can support true WoW and MoM growth tracking once we start persisting snapshots.

provider_snapshots.measurement: Direct tokens, token throughput, MAU, and developer adoption
provider_snapshots.headline: Google is the cleanest big-lab source for direct token throughput disclosures.
bullets: Google gives the strongest first-party token metrics among the major labs.
The jump from 9.7T to 480T+ to 980T+ monthly tokens is one of the clearest public inference-demand curves in the market.
Direct API throughput and MAU let us separate developer demand from consumer adoption.

sources.label: Alphabet Q3 2025 message from the CEO
Alphabet Q4 2025 message from the CEO

provider_snapshots.measurement: Messages, users, seats, and estimated token-equivalent throughput
provider_snapshots.headline: OpenAI discloses scale through message counts and user growth more than token totals.

estimated_tokens.method: Directional estimate only: multiply 2.627B daily messages by 300 to 1,500 total tokens per message to represent a consumer-to-heavy-workload band.
estimated_tokens.warning: Use as a framing range, not a precise valuation input.

bullets: OpenAI is enormous on user and message volume, but still relatively sparse on direct token-throughput disclosures.
Message growth and seat growth make OpenAI one of the best demand proxies even when the token math must be estimated.
The token-equivalent range is directionally useful for comparing scale with disclosed token metrics from Google and OpenRouter.

sources.label: ChatGPT usage and adoption patterns at work

provider_snapshots.measurement: Usage-composition proxies and marketplace token floors
provider_snapshots.headline: Anthropic publishes strong usage-quality data, but not direct system-wide token totals.

current_metric.label: OpenRouter floor
current_metric.growth: Observed from Claude Sonnet 4.6 plus Claude Opus 4.6 on OpenRouter rankings.

rows.metric: Occupations with AI use in at least 25% of tasks
Occupations with AI use in at least 75% of tasks

bullets: Anthropic is excellent for seeing what Claude is used for, especially the shift toward coding and enterprise automation.
For total-demand sizing, Anthropic needs to be treated as a partial-information case.
The best near-term approach is to track Claude's OpenRouter token floor and pair it with Anthropic's official Economic Index updates.
